title: Asynchronous Architecture Decisions for Go Services · Runtime Contract: Broker Fit Boundary
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must make coupling, buffering, fan-out, replay, and failure costs observable at Go service boundaries; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the broker fit boundary increment in a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Choose choreography or orchestration by workflow visibility, policy ownership, compensation, evolution, and failure diagnosis needs; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 7462 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration.
